title: Distribution of the Estimators for Autoregressive Time Series with a Unit Root
venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
summary: Introduces the Dickey-Fuller unit-root framework and asymptotic distributions for testing nonstationarity; critical for checking whether lottery feature time series are regime-stable before bias inference.
key_equations: Delta y_t = gamma y_{t-1} + e_t, gamma = rho-1
assumptions: AR(1)-style data-generating process
Innovations are independent with finite variance
contributions: Derivation of nonstandard null distributions for unit-root tests
claims: Classical t-distributions are invalid under unit-root null
conclusions: Dedicated critical values are required for unit-root inference
limitations: Baseline formulation assumes simple AR structure

title: Testing for Unit Roots in Autoregressive-Moving Average Models of Unknown Order
summary: Extends Dickey-Fuller testing to higher-order ARMA error structures using augmentation; directly relevant for dependence checks in lottery-derived feature sequences.
key_equations: Delta y_t = alpha y_{t-1} + sum_{i=1}^p beta_i Delta y_{t-i} + e_t
assumptions: Lag augmentation captures short-run dependence
claims: Augmentation yields valid unit-root tests under broader dynamics

title: Testing the null hypothesis of stationarity against the alternative of a unit root
summary: Introduces KPSS stationarity test, complementary to ADF. Useful for symmetric evidence: rejecting both nulls flags modeling misspecification or structural instability.
assumptions: Long-run variance estimator is consistent
claims: Provides complementary evidence to unit-root-null tests
evidence: citation:Kwiatkowski et al. (1992). Journal of Econometrics 54(1-3):159-178.

title: Testing for a Unit Root in Time Series Regression
summary: Introduces nonparametric corrections for serial correlation and heteroskedasticity in unit-root testing; relevant when lottery features have unknown error dynamics.
assumptions: Weak dependence and consistent long-run variance estimation
contributions: PP test statistics robust to nuisance autocorrelation
claims: No lag augmentation required for correction
conclusions: Alternative to ADF under complex errors

title: On a Measure of Lack of Fit in Time Series Models
summary: Defines Ljung-Box Q-statistic for residual autocorrelation; central for testing independence assumptions in draw-level feature models.
assumptions: Model residuals are approximately white noise under null
claims: Improved small-sample behavior versus Box-Pierce

title: Distribution of Residual Autocorrelations in Autoregressive-Integrated Moving Average Time Series Models
venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
summary: Establishes Box-Pierce residual autocorrelation test, a precursor to Ljung-Box; useful as baseline independence diagnostic.
assumptions: Large-sample approximation
claims: Enables compact model adequacy testing
conclusions: Residual whiteness can be tested jointly

title: A test for independence based on the correlation dimension
summary: BDS test detects nonlinear dependence missed by linear autocorrelation tests; relevant for checking subtle mechanism-driven structure in lottery features.
assumptions: IID null after prewhitening/modeling
claims: Can reject IID when linear tests do not
limitations: Sensitive to embedding dimension and epsilon

title: Continuous Inspection Schemes
summary: Introduces CUSUM sequential change detection; supports rolling/local anomaly monitoring around operational lottery transitions.
assumptions: Reference in-control distribution specified
claims: CUSUM is sensitive to small persistent shifts

title: Techniques for Testing the Constancy of Regression Relationships Over Time
venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series B
summary: Develops CUSUM and CUSUMSQ stability diagnostics for regression coefficients; relevant to testing whether estimated lottery effects persist across eras.
assumptions: Recursive residual framework
claims: Detects gradual and abrupt instability

title: Estimating and Testing Linear Models with Multiple Structural Changes
summary: Provides estimation/testing for multiple structural breaks with unknown dates; directly applicable to rule/machine transition analysis.
key_equations: y_t = x_t' beta_j + u_t for t=T_{j-1}+1,...,T_j
assumptions: Piecewise linear model with finite number of breaks
claims: Consistent estimation of multiple breakpoints
limitations: Computational cost increases with candidate breaks

title: Computation and analysis of multiple structural change models
summary: Expands practical computation and inference for multiple-break models; useful for robust era splitting prior to hypothesis tests.
assumptions: Segment-wise parametric structure
contributions: Algorithms and tests for break detection
claims: Feasible applied workflow for multiple breaks
evidence: citation:Bai, J., Perron, P. (2003). J. Applied Econometrics 18(1):1-22.

title: Optimal Detection of Changepoints With a Linear Computational Cost
venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
summary: Introduces PELT algorithm for exact penalized changepoint detection in near-linear time; crucial for long lottery archives.
assumptions: Additive segment cost and penalty
claims: Substantially faster than naive exact dynamic programming

title: changepoint: An R Package for Changepoint Analysis
summary: Operationalizes changepoint methods (including PELT) in reproducible software; relevant for transparent experiment pipelines.
key_equations: min_{m,	au} \{sum_{j=0}^{m} C(y_{	au_j+1:	au_{j+1}}) + beta f(m)\}
assumptions: Segment-wise parametric cost models
contributions: Reference implementation for multiple CPD algorithms
claims: Accessible tooling improves applied changepoint practice

title: Selective review of offline change point detection methods
summary: Systematic review of offline CPD methods and tradeoffs; helps choose robust break-detection families for lottery-bias studies.
assumptions: Piecewise stationarity/parametric segments depending on method
claims: No single CPD method dominates all settings
conclusions: Method choice should track signal/noise and cost model
limitations: Review scope excludes some online methods

title: Controlling the False Discovery Rate: A Practical and Powerful Approach to Multiple Testing
summary: Introduces BH procedure controlling FDR, foundational for scanning large combinatorial feature spaces without uncontrolled false positives.
assumptions: Independent or positively dependent tests for exact control
contributions: FDR concept and practical step-up algorithm
claims: Higher power than FWER controls in large-scale testing
conclusions: Suitable default correction for broad scans
limitations: Under arbitrary dependence can be anti-conservative

title: The control of the false discovery rate in multiple testing under dependency
summary: Extends FDR control to general dependence (BY correction), providing conservative safeguards when lottery features are correlated.
assumptions: Arbitrary dependency structures
claims: Controls FDR under broad dependence assumptions
evidence: citation:Benjamini, Y., Yekutieli, D. (2001). Ann. Stat. 29(4):1165-1188.

title: A direct approach to false discovery rates
summary: Introduces q-values and direct FDR estimation via pi0, enabling more powerful large-scale inference pipelines.
assumptions: Valid p-values; estimable proportion of true nulls
claims: Can improve power relative to fixed-threshold BH

title: Statistical significance for genomewide studies
summary: Demonstrates q-value methodology at genome scale; transferable to lottery feature scans where hypotheses are numerous.
assumptions: Large-scale hypothesis setting with valid p-values
claims: q-values provide interpretable false-discovery calibration

title: Large-Scale Simultaneous Hypothesis Testing: The Choice of a Null Hypothesis
summary: Analyzes empirical-null choices in massive testing, useful when lottery-derived statistics show calibration drift from theoretical nulls.
assumptions: Mixture model for test statistics
claims: Theoretical null can miscalibrate in practice

title: A Statistical Test Suite for Random and Pseudorandom Number Generators for Cryptographic Applications
summary: Reference battery of randomness tests; provides a transferable template for binary-sequence diagnostics derived from lottery encodings.
key_equations: Uniformity test via chi^2 across P-value bins
assumptions: Statistical tests are screening tools, not proof of cryptographic security
claims: No finite battery can certify true randomness
evidence: citation:Bassham et al. (2010). NIST SP 800-22 Rev.1a.

title: TestU01: A C Library for Empirical Testing of Random Number Generators
summary: Introduces TestU01 batteries (SmallCrush/Crush/BigCrush); useful as high-power alternatives when testing transformed lottery sequences.
key_equations: U_i = F(X_i) for uniformization under null
assumptions: Generator outputs mapped to U(0,1) under null
contributions: Comprehensive RNG testing library and methodology
claims: Different generators fail different deep tests
evidence: citation:L'Ecuyer, P., Simard, R. (2007). ACM TOMACS 17(4).

title: Mersenne Twister: A 623-dimensionally equidistributed uniform pseudorandom number generator
summary: Canonical PRNG baseline used in Monte Carlo null simulations for lottery studies.
assumptions: Finite-state linear recurrence over F2
claims: Improved quality/speed tradeoff vs earlier generators
evidence: citation:Matsumoto, M., Nishimura, T. (1998). ACM TOMACS 8(1):3-30.

title: Random numbers fall mainly in the planes
summary: Classic spectral warning for linear congruential generators; motivates robust RNG choices for null simulations in lottery analysis.
assumptions: Linear congruential form
contributions: Geometric diagnosis of RNG lattice artifacts
claims: Many LCGs exhibit low-dimensional structure
conclusions: RNG selection materially affects inference quality

title: Statistical Testing of Random Number Generators and Their Improvement Using Randomness Extraction
summary: Evaluates RNGs with multiple test batteries and studies extractor-based post-processing; directly relevant for robust randomness diagnostics methodology.
assumptions: Min-entropy estimation and extractor assumptions are valid
contributions: Comparative battery testing + extractor impact analysis
claims: Post-processing can significantly improve test outcomes
conclusions: Entropy assumptions should be explicit and conservative
limitations: Extractor performance depends on source assumptions
future_work: Broader RNG classes and extraction pipelines

title: A Scale-Free Approach for False Discovery Rate Control in Generalized Linear Models
summary: Develops mirror-statistic approach for FDR control in GLMs; useful for count/binary response models in feature-selection stages.
assumptions: Asymptotic symmetry of mirror statistics under null
claims: Improved finite-sample robustness in GLMs
conclusions: Alternative to BH/knockoffs in GLM settings

title: A robust false discovery rate controlling procedure using the empirical likelihood with a fast algorithm
venue: Journal of Statistical Computation and Simulation
summary: Proposes empirical-likelihood-based FDR control under non-normal nulls; relevant when lottery feature statistics are non-Gaussian.
assumptions: Empirical likelihood constraints encode null adequately
contributions: Robust FDR control with fast algorithm
claims: Better robustness vs strict parametric-null methods
conclusions: Useful sensitivity analysis for null misspecification

title: Multiscale Change Point Detection for Univariate Time Series Data with Missing Value
summary: Combines imputation and multiscale changepoint testing under missingness; relevant if operational metadata gaps are present.
assumptions: State-space imputation reasonably recovers latent process
claims: Improved changepoint localization with imputation

title: Multivariate Time Series Change-Point Detection with a Novel Pearson-like Scaled Bregman Divergence
summary: Introduces divergence-based multivariate CPD; relevant for joint monitoring of multiple lottery descriptors.
assumptions: Piecewise distributional regimes in multivariate streams
claims: Competitive detection accuracy in multivariate settings
conclusions: Useful when single-feature scans miss joint shifts

title: Investigating Randomness and Fairness in the Romanian 6/49 Lottery
summary: Direct lottery-domain empirical study using chi-square and Monte Carlo on Romanian 6/49 draws; useful external benchmark for effect-size expectations.
assumptions: Uniform draw null under game rules
claims: No statistically significant global frequency bias at alpha=0.05
conclusions: Observed frequencies broadly consistent with randomness
limitations: Focuses on marginal frequencies, limited mechanism variables

title: Lottery Numbers and Ordered Statistics
summary: Applies ordered-statistics framing to lottery numbers; informs rank-based descriptors that are invariant to permutation.
assumptions: Underlying draws follow discrete-uniform sampling without replacement
claims: Order-based views can clarify expected structure
limitations: Limited inferential depth on operational biases
future_work: Integrate with dependence and regime tests

title: Noise-Rejecting Photonic Integrated Circuit for Robust Quantum Random Number Generation
summary: Presents high-speed QRNG architecture with built-in noise rejection; useful as modern reference for physical randomness generation constraints.
assumptions: Quantum-noise-dominant entropy source after calibration
contributions: Integrated photonic QRNG design with robust extraction
claims: High throughput with stability under practical noise
conclusions: Hardware randomness can be miniaturized at scale

title: Gambling Commission annual report and accounts 2024 to 2025
summary: Regulatory report for UK gambling oversight period 2024-2025; provides governance and operational context for lottery integrity discussions.
assumptions: Regulator-reported figures reflect audited operations
contributions: Recent regulatory context and accountability documentation
claims: Publishes annual operational/accountability statements
conclusions: Useful governance context for system-level fairness discussions
limitations: Not a dedicated statistical fairness study of draw outcomes
evidence: citation:UK Gambling Commission (2025). Annual report and accounts 2024 to 2025.

title: Allwyn 2024 Annual Report and Accounts
summary: Operator-level annual report covering 2024 financial/operational highlights; useful for current operational context and governance signals.
assumptions: Company disclosures accurately summarize operations
contributions: Current operator context including digital and operational metrics
claims: Reports 2024 revenue/EBITDA and strategy execution
limitations: Corporate report, not independent statistical audit

title: Powerball official game documentation and result publications
summary: Official game-rule and draw-publication source for U.S. Powerball; candidate external dataset for cross-system robustness tests.
assumptions: Published numbers are official and independently audited
contributions: Canonical operational and results source for Powerball
claims: Provides official draw/result information
limitations: Web extraction pipeline needed for structured historical table
evidence: citation:Multi-State Lottery Association (2026). Powerball official website.

title: LOTTO 6aus49 Spielregeln
summary: Official German 6aus49 rule specification with draw structure and schedules; required for precise null-model formulation for k-of-n + bonus mechanics.
assumptions: Rule page accurately reflects current format
contributions: Primary rules source for German lottery format
claims: Defines draw mechanism and prize classes
conclusions: Supports exact combinatorial nulls for the attached German dataset

title: The National Lottery Lotto draw details (example draw records)
summary: Official UK draw-history pages include draw machine and ball set tags, useful for mechanism-specific covariates and change-point annotation.
assumptions: Draw-history pages are complete and accurate
claims: Includes draw machine and ball set identifiers
conclusions: Useful external benchmark for mechanism covariates
limitations: Programmatic fetch intermittently unstable (502 from mirror host)
future_work: Use stable host and archival snapshots
evidence: citation:The National Lottery (2025). Lotto draw history pages.

title: ruptures: change point detection in Python
summary: Widely used Python CPD toolkit implementing PELT, binary segmentation, and kernel models; strong candidate for reproducible regime-analysis code.
assumptions: Library implementations match referenced methods
claims: Supports modular offline CPD workflows
conclusions: High relevance for this project’s regime segmentation

title: arcetri/sts: Improved NIST Statistical Test Suite
summary: Improved open implementation of NIST STS with batch/distributed improvements; useful for reproducible binary-randomness battery testing.
assumptions: Implementation-level fixes align with documented test definitions
claims: Improves usability and performance over legacy STS
conclusions: Good candidate for reproducible randomness-test execution
limitations: Still dependent on careful input-bitstream preparation

title: Historical lotto draws dataset (1986-2026) user-provided attachment
summary: Primary empirical dataset spanning 1986-09-07 to 2026-03-15 with 6-number outcomes, bonus fields, and source provenance; core data for all downstream analyses.
assumptions: Merged source files were transcribed correctly
contributions: Longitudinal draw corpus for regime and bias testing
claims: Contains draw date, six main numbers, bonus and payout fields
conclusions: Sufficient temporal depth for rolling/local and global tests
limitations: No direct ball-mass or operator-level latent variables
